questions.question: Where is the ZAP API UI available when proxying via ZAP?
questions.explanation: The API UI is accessible via http://zap/ when proxying through ZAP.

questions.question: Which ZAP mode only allows potentially dangerous actions on URLs in scope?
questions.explanation: Protected mode restricts potentially dangerous actions to in-scope URLs.

questions.question: Which ZAP mode actively scans new in-scope nodes as soon as they are discovered?
questions.explanation: Attack mode triggers active scanning automatically for new in-scope nodes.

questions.question: In ZAP, scope is defined primarily by what?
questions.explanation: Scope is defined by the contexts you set in ZAP.

questions.question: Contexts in ZAP are defined using what to match URLs?
questions.explanation: Contexts use regex patterns that must match full URLs.

questions.question: Users in ZAP are closely tied to which features?
questions.explanation: Users rely on the authentication and session management defined in a context.

questions.question: Quick Start Automated Scan requires you to enter what?
questions.explanation: Automated Scan launches against the URL you specify.

questions.question: The traditional spider is fast but struggles with what type of applications?
questions.explanation: Traditional spider finds links in HTML and does not handle heavy JavaScript well.

questions.question: The AJAX spider explores applications by using what?
questions.explanation: AJAX spider launches a browser and clicks links to explore JS-driven apps.

questions.question: When using -zapit, which option must be included or it will be ignored?
questions.explanation: ZAPit requires -cmd or the option is ignored.

summary: Check your understanding of zap advanced concepts.
title: ZAP Advanced Quiz 135
